zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen於1215年出生在德國薩辛可萊斯 梅斯時，他的父親Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen53歲，母親Jutta von Thüringen11212歲。他於1234年5月1日與Constance von Babenberg在奧地利維恩結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1288年2月15日在德國薩辛德累斯頓享年73歲，埋葬在瑞典索德曼藍德可洛斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen于1215年出生在德国薩辛可萊斯 梅斯时，他的父亲Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen53岁，母亲Jutta von Thüringen11212岁。他于1234年5月1日与Constance von Babenberg在奥地利維恩结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1288年2月15日在德国薩辛德累斯頓享年73岁，埋葬在瑞典索德曼藍德可洛斯特。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Meißen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Ernst.
North German and Dutch: patronymic from   Mein   1.

variants.name: Mein
meanings: German and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Maino, Meino, a short form of various compound names with the first element magin ‘strength, might’, for example Meinhardt . Compare Main 7.
German: nickname from Middle High German mein ‘mean, nasty’.

Scottish and English: perhaps a variant of Main or Meen. Meen is either a nickname from Middle English mene ‘inferior, lowly; poor; unpleasant’, from the Middle English personal name Mene (which is of uncertain origin). Alternatively, it could conceivably be a habitational name from East and West Meon (Hampshire), which take their names from the river Meon (of unexplained etymology).

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: III.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen was born in 1215, in Meißen, Saxony, Germany, his father, Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen, was 53 and his mother, Jutta von Thüringen, was 11212. He married Constance von Babenberg on 1 May 1234, in Vienna, Austria. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 15 February 1288, in Dresden, Saxony, Germany, at the age of 73, and was buried in Kloster, Södermanland, Sweden.

fr.Of Maltitz, Meissen, Sachsen, Germany: Maltitz, Maltitz bei Nossen, Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Allemagne
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen est né en 1215, à Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Allemagne, son père, Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Jutta von Thüringen, avait 11212 ans. Il a épousé Constance von Babenberg le 1 mai 1234, à Vienne, Autriche. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 15 février 1288, à Dresden, Sachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Kloster, Södermanland, Suède.

de.Of Maltitz, Meissen, Sachsen, Germany: Maltitz, Maltitz bei Nossen, Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen 1215 in Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen 53 und seine Mutter Jutta von Thüringen 11212. Er hat am 1. Mai 1234 in Wien, Österreich Constance von Babenberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 15. Februar 1288 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Dresden, Sachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Kloster, Södermanland, Schweden begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen è nato il nel 1215 nel Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Germania, il suo padre, Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen, aveva 53 anni e il suo madre, Jutta von Thüringen, aveva 11212 anni. Lui sposato Constance von Babenberg il 1 maggio 1234, nel Wien, Austria. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 15 febbraio 1288, nel Dresden, Sachsen, Germania, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a Kloster, Södermanland, Svezia.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißenが1215年にドイツサチセンクレイス メイssンで生まれた時，父Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißenは53歳，母Jutta von Thüringenは11212歳であった。1234年5月1日にオーストリアウィンでConstance von Babenbergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1288年2月15日にドイツサチセンドレスデンで73歳で死去し，スウェーデンスoデルマンランドクロスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Of Maltitz, Meissen, Sachsen, Germany: 독일 작센 크레이스 이ss 맬티츠 브이 노센 맬티츠
ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen(이)가 1215년 독일 작센 크레이스 이ss에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen(은)는 53세였고, 그의 어머니 Jutta von Thüringen(은)는 11212세였다. 그는 1234년 5월 1일 오스트리아 빈에서 Constance von Babenberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1288년 2월 15일 독일 작센 드레즈든에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 스웨덴 스o더먼랜드 클로스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen nasceu em 1215, em Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Alemanha, seu pai, Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Jutta von Thüringen, tinha 11212 anos. Ele casou-se com Constance von Babenberg em 1 de maio de 1234, em Wien, Áustria. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 15 de fevereiro de 1288, em Dresden, Sachsen, Alemanha, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em Kloster, Södermanland, Suécia.

ro.Of Maltitz, Meissen, Sachsen, Germany: Maltitz, Maltitz bei Nossen, Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Germania

ru.Of Maltitz, Meissen, Sachsen, Germany: малтиц, малтиц би носсен, крис миssен, сачсен, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen родился в 1215 г. в местности крис миssен, сачсен, Германия, его отцу по имени Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Jutta von Thüringen, 11212. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance von Babenberg 1 мая 1234 г. в местности Вена, Австрия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 15 февраля 1288 г. в местности дресден, сачсен, Германия в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности клостир, сoдирманланд, Швеция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich III. der Erlauchte von Meißen nació en 1215, en Kreis Meißen, Sachsen, Alemania, su papá, Dietrich der Bedrängte von Meißen, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Jutta von Thüringen, tenía 11212 años. Se casó con Constance von Babenberg el 1 de mayo de 1234, en Wien, Austria. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 15 de febrero de 1288, en Dresden, Sachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Kloster, Södermanland, Suecia.
